page: Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6a.2xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2992.19 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the average range, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6393.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 13110.28. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 567.48 GB/sec for cached small blocks, though uncached large-block write bandwidth is relatively weak at 22.31 GB/sec. The processor supports AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, which assist in cryptographic and integer manipulation tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c6a.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for dedicated x86_64 workloads without the overhead of bundled local storage. It is well-suited for compute-bound workloads such as batch processing, web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, the 16.0 GB memory limit and weak large-block write bandwidth make it less optimal for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. It delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, alongside average multi-core and memory read capabilities, though large-block memory write speeds are relatively weak. Supported instruction sets include AVX, AES, and SHA_NI. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and distributed analytics that do not require high memory density or local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, designed for compute-heavy workloads on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory bandwidth for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps with no local storage
Optimized for web serving, software compilation, and batch processing
